channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Bolivia's divisive popular vote for senior judges offers lessons for Mexico
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — Campaigning in Bolivia for Sunday's judicial election may be strictly forbidden, but look closer on the streets of the capital La Paz and you’ll find that some candidates have sneakily plastered their faces on packs of corn puffs and others have slipped subtle slogans into off
item.dc:creator: PAOLA FLORES and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Death toll in French territory of Mayotte from Cyclone Chido is 'several hundred,' top official says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — The death toll in the French territory of Mayotte from Cyclone Chido is “several hundred” and may run into the thousands, the island’s top government official told a local broadcaster Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel will close its Ireland embassy over Gaza tensions as Palestinian death toll nears 45,000
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel said Sunday it will close its embassy in Ireland as relations deteriorated over the war in Gaza, where Palestinian medical officials said new Israeli airstrikes killed over 30 people including several children.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oil spill in Kerch Strait after two Russian oil tankers seriously damaged in storm
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Two Russian oil tankers have been seriously damaged following a storm in the Kerch Strait, spilling oil and sparking an emergency rescue operation, Russian officials told state news outlets Sunday. Some reports said at least one of the vessels later sank.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Chronicling the Assad regime from death of the father to defeat of the son
item.description: Fox News senior foreign affairs correspondent Greg Palkot documents his years of reporting from Syria in the aftermath of the fall of the regime of Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oil spill in Kerch Strait after two Russian oil tankers seriously damaged in storm
item.description: At least one person has died after two Russian oil tankers took heavy storm damage in the Kerch Strait, spilling oil and sparking an emergency rescue operation, Russian officials told state news outlets.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Christian leader in Lebanon urges US, allies to intervene to stop Hezbollah
item.description: The leader of a Christian coalition and political party in Lebanon is calling on the U.S. and its Western allies to deploy troops to Lebanon to help the army dismantle Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump appoints longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as special missions envoy
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has picked longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as an envoy for special missions.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — American officials have been in direct contact with the terrorist-designated rebel group that led the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad, U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Saturday. Blinken, speaking at a news conference in Jordan, was the first U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US reiterates support for South Korea after impeachment of president
item.description: The United States reiterated its support for South Korea, an ally in East Asia, and vowed to advance mutual interests after the country’s president Yoon Suk Yeol was impeached by the parliament.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Filipino woman on Indonesia death row recalls a stunning last minute reprieve and 'miracle' transfer
item.description: YOGYAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Filipino death row inmate Mary Jane Fiesta Veloso knelt to pray when officers came to take her to an execution site in May 2015, just a few feet away from her isolation cell on an Indonesian prison island, where a 13-member firing squad was waiting.
item.dc:creator: SLAMET RIYADI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.), who was hospitalized during a trip to Luxembourg, underwent hip surgery early Saturday, according to a spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: South Korea lawmakers vote to impeach president over martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean lawmakers on Saturday voted to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived martial law declaration earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman arrested for attempting to smuggle 22 pounds of meth wrapped as Christmas gifts in carry-on bag
item.description: A 29-year-old woman was arrested after she allegedly attempted to smuggle methamphetamine wrapped as Christmas presents in her carry-on bag.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: As the newly-named host of the 2034 World Cup in men's soccer, Saudi Arabia says it will construct or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US military flies American released from Syrian prison to Jordan, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has transported out of Syria an American who had disappeared seven months ago into former President Bashar Assad’s notorious prison system and was among the thousands released this week by rebels, U.S. officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American freed from Syrian prison after Assad's overthrow taken out of country by US military
item.description: Travis Timmerman, an American who was freed by Syrian rebels this week after the overthrow of former President Bashar al-Assad, has been flown out of the country by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US military flies missing American out of Syria
item.description: The U.S. military on Friday flew a missing American citizen out of Syria this week, after the stunning fall of Syrian leader Bashar Assad's regime. Travis Timmerman was transferred to U.S. forces in Syria on Friday, according to a defense official, and was flown to Jordan. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Hearings are done: Now the wait begins for verdict on closely-watched climate case at UN’s top court
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A lawyer for a small South Pacific island nation told the world’s highest court to heed her warning.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel eyes Iran nuke sites amid reports Trump mulls moves to block Tehran atomic program
item.description: Israel is once again said to be eyeing strikes on Iran's nuclear program as the incoming Trump administration is reportedly mulling plans on how to come down hard on Iran without prompting a war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China's military is staying silent on its recent activity around Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's military kept silent Friday on its large deployment of naval and coast guard ships off Taiwan this week, with its top spokesperson quoting an ancient strategist instead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey drones: Sheriff calls for state of emergency
item.description: Shaun Golden, the sheriff of Monmouth County, New Jersey, says that more government assistance is needed to identify the drones.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Indian teen Gukesh Dommaraju becomes the youngest chess world champion after beating Chinese rival
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Indian teenager Gukesh Dommaraju became the youngest chess world champion Thursday after beating the defending champion Ding Liren of China in the final match of their series in Singapore.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top US ally, SDF commander in Syria warns of ISIS return if Turkish airstrikes don’t stop
item.description: Gen. Mazloum Abdi, the commander of the Syrian Democratic Forces, said the Turkish military continue to attack his Kurdish forces, despite a U.S. brokered ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Travis Timmerman, the missing American apparently found in Syria
item.description: An American man found wandering the streets outside the Syrian capital of Damascus barefoot identified himself as Pete Travis Timmerman on Thursday. Timmerman was last seen in Budapest, Hungary, in June and had not been heard from in months.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Jake Sullivan says Netanyahu 'ready to do a deal' as Hamas said to concede to Israel cease-fire demands
item.description: Hamas and Israel have signaled they may be taking more serious steps to reach a cease-fire and to secure the release of the hostages held in Gaza for more than a year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO chief warns of 'huge risk' in Ukraine talks without Putin
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte expressed concern about the idea of conducting peace negotiations in the nearly three-year-long Russia-Ukraine war without Russian President Vladimir Putin "at the table," calling the move "risky.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump has invited Xi Jinping to attend inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump has invited Chinese President Xi Jinping to attend his inauguration next month in Washington, a spokesperson confirmed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Arabica coffee sold for $3.44 a pound on the global market earlier this week, beating the prior record high of $3.35 set in 1977.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: The world’s largest Christmas tree farm in Oregon sells nearly 1 million trees annually
item.description: Oregon produces more Christmas trees than any other U.S. state, with 4.7 million harvested every year. The state produces 33% of the United States' Christmas trees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Journalists' rights group says 104 media workers killed this year
item.description: More than half of the journalists and media workers killed perished during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's Justice Ministry imposes a travel ban on President Yoon over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Justice Ministry on Monday imposed an overseas travel ban on President Yoon Suk Yeol as authorities investigate allegations of rebellion and other charges in connection with his short-lived declaration of martial law last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
